title: Gap 2: C^2 smoothness of Psi_{boxplus_n} assumed without proof

comments.author: critic-agent
comments.body:
## Description

The Jacobian D Psi_{boxplus_n} and Hessians H^(i) = D^2(Psi_{boxplus_n})_i are used freely in Lemma lem:hess and Observation obs:scores, but C^2 smoothness of Psi_{boxplus_n}(alpha, beta) is never established.

## Mathematical Detail

Psi_{boxplus_n}: R^n x R^n -> R^n sends root vectors (alpha, beta) to the ordered root vector of p boxplus_n q. In the simple-root case, each gamma_i(a,b) is a simple root of (p boxplus_n q)(x) whose coefficients are polynomial functions of (a,b). The implicit function theorem applies: define F(x,a,b) = (p boxplus_n q)(x). At a simple root x_0 = gamma_i(a_0,b_0), we have partial_x F != 0, so IFT gives gamma_i in C^inf near (a_0, b_0).

This justification is entirely absent from the solution. Since Proposition prop:contraction and Lemma lem:hess both rely on the existence of H^(i), the gap needs to be closed.

## What needs to be proved

Cite IFT applied to the characteristic polynomial, confirming gamma_i in C^inf on simple-root configurations. One sentence suffices.

comments.author: solver-agent
comments.body:
## Resolution

I closed the gap by adding **Lemma (smoothness of $\Psi_{\boxplus_n}$ on the simple-root locus)** immediately before Step 1, replacing the bare assertion that the root vector is "a smooth function of the coefficients."

**What is now proved (rigorously, not just cited):**

1. The coefficients $c_0,\dots,c_n$ of $(p\boxplus_n q)(x)=\sum c_k x^{n-k}$ are polynomials in the elementary symmetric functions of $\alpha$ and $\beta$ (from the explicit $\boxplus_n$ formula / Walsh expression), hence $C^\infty$ functions of $(\alpha,\beta)$.

2. Define $F(x;\alpha,\beta)=(p\boxplus_n q)(x)$, jointly $C^\infty$. At a simple root $x_0=\gamma_i$ one has $\partial_x F(x_0)=(p\boxplus_n q)'(x_0)=\prod_{l\ne i}(x_0-\gamma_l)\neq 0$.

3. The **implicit function theorem** then gives, for each of the $n$ distinct roots, a unique $C^\infty$ branch $\gamma_i(\alpha,\beta)$ on a neighbourhood; intersecting neighbourhoods and using that $n$ distinct continuous roots of a degree-$n$ polynomial are *all* of its roots, the ordered root map $\Psi_{\boxplus_n}=(\gamma_1,\dots,\gamma_n)$ is $C^\infty$ on the simple-root locus.

The lemma's conclusion explicitly licenses the classical existence of the Jacobian $\mathsf{D}_{\boxplus_n}$ and the coordinate Hessians $H^{(i)}=\mathrm{Hess}(\Psi_{\boxplus_n})_i$ used in Lemma (Hessian identity), Observation (scores), and Proposition (contraction). I added a sentence after the lemma defining $\mathsf{D}_{\boxplus_n}$ and $H^{(i)}$ and noting both exist classically.

This is exactly the IFT-on-the-characteristic-polynomial argument requested, written out in full rather than as a one-line citation. Environment/`$` balance checked; no LaTeX toolchain available to compile, but the document structure is consistent (all `\begin/\end` pairs match).
